findings: Distinctive editorial voice: oversized serif typography, deep green, citrus accent, and terracotta create a memorable nutrition brand.
Exceptional full-page investment. Planning interface, nutrition dashboard, testimonial metrics, CTA, and substantial footer all receive bespoke treatment.
Layout variety is purposeful: asymmetric hero, numbered cards, product preview, dark analytical split, editorial testimonial, and contrasting CTA.
Food photography is repeatedly integrated with overlays, data, cropping, and product storytelling—not used as decoration alone.
Mobile meaningfully recomposes complex desktop sections and maintains strong typography, imagery, and section identity.
Minor deductions: the hero is visually dense, some mobile copy is small, and the horizontally clipped weekday navigation slightly weakens clarity.
Highly polished, calm editorial direction with confident typography and a credible wellness palette.
Strong hierarchy throughout, though generous whitespace and repeated heading-plus-card structures make portions feel more conventional than Version A.
Lower sections remain carefully designed: nutrition guidance, sample week, testimonial/stat block, CTA, and footer are all complete.
Photography supports product concepts through meal-plan and nutrition overlays, but repeated bowl imagery reduces visual breadth.
Mobile adaptation is clear and well ordered, with complex modules successfully stacked and preserved.
Minor deductions: repeated cream/green panels create a more predictable rhythm, the three-step section feels comparatively sparse, and several mobile tabs/content rows visibly clip at the edge.

limitations: Assessment is based solely on the four supplied still screenshots. Interaction behavior, animation, accessibility semantics, loading states, and actual breakpoint behavior cannot be verified.
